meta.goal: A diverse, deterministically-scorable synthetic problem taxonomy to train a model that generalizes across the whole 'LLM writes code to optimize a scored objective' space: competitive/combinatorial optimization (FrontierCS/ALE-Bench), evolutionary program search (FunSearch/AlphaEvolve/OpenEvolve/ThetaEvolve/TTT-Discover), engineering optimization (Frontier-Eng), ML-engineering (MLS-Bench), and evaluation-driven scientific discovery (SimpleTES).
hard_rules: Every task is scored by a DETERMINISTIC, offline, CPU-only evaluator: exact integer arithmetic, seeded numpy, combinatorial checker, or op-counter.
The score is the OBJECTIVE VALUE (or a normalized ratio of it), never wall-clock time or hardware throughput. Any time/memory limit is only a VALIDITY GATE, not the reward.
The evaluator RE-COMPUTES the objective from the raw solution artifact and never trusts a self-reported score (ThetaEvolve anti-cheat pattern). Feasibility violation -> score 0 / large penalty.
Difficulty scales by PARAMETER (n, d, N, field, graph family, seed, discretization), not by problem identity, so a small instance gives fast reward and larger instances test generalization.
Prefer instance FAMILIES over single record instances (no train-on-the-test-problem); reserve famous open-problem instances (cap-set n=8, Hadamard n=29) for occasional eval only.
meta.generalization_thesis: One shared skill underlies all frameworks: 'propose an explicit artifact (program/heuristic/construction/expression/schedule) subject to a hard feasibility checker, then improve a scalar objective the checker recomputes.' Training over the union of these formats and families teaches that construct-and-verify-and-refine skill rather than memorized constructions.

A.name: testlib instance-based combinatorial optimization (C++)
A.when_to_use: NP-hard / open-ended combinatorial optimization whose objective is a validity-gated quality ratio over FIXED instances. The benchmark home format: FrontierCS algorithmic (non-interactive 'default' problems), ALE-Bench AHC (as static, budget-fixed instances), Frontier-Eng JobShop. Model submits a C++ program run on frozen testdata.
file_contract: statement.txt  -- markdown: Title, Problem, Input, Output, Feasibility, Objective, Scoring, Constraints (time+memory), Example (with worked score)
gen.cpp        -- testlib generator; testId 1..10 is a difficulty/structure ladder (tiny->large/adversarial), deterministic via testlib rnd; each .in <= ~20MB, generates <40s

chk.cc         -- testlib checker: strict feasibility via bounded reads (ouf.readInt(lo,hi)) + seekEof; compute objective F and an INTERNAL baseline B the checker builds itself (B>0); emit 'Ratio: <float>' via quitp
config.yaml    -- {checker: chk.cc, memory, subtasks:[{n_cases,score}], time, type: default}
testdata/*.in  -- generated frozen inputs
solutions/{trivial,greedy,strong,invalid}.cpp  -- ladder; first line '// TIER: <name>'
meta.json, validation.json
A.deterministic_scoring: Checker validates feasibility (infeasible/garbage -> 0), computes objective, normalizes: minimization sc=100*B/max(1,F), maximization sc=100*F/max(1,B), capped at 10x baseline; report Ratio=sc/1000 in [0,1]. Fixed testdata + deterministic O(input) checker => reproducible. Time limit is ONLY a validity gate. Audited by the existing 8-gate harness (G1 compile ... G8 divergence).

B.name: Python program/heuristic evolution against a deterministic evaluator
B.when_to_use: FunSearch/AlphaEvolve/OpenEvolve/ThetaEvolve/TTT-Discover/SimpleTES style. The artifact is an ALGORITHM/HEURISTIC/POLICY (not a single static object): model writes ONE small function inside a frozen scaffold; a fixed evaluator RUNS it over an instance family and recomputes the scalar. Use for heuristic evolution (priority/dispatch rules, online algorithms), constructor programs, and engineering/ML objectives computed by a CPU model.
file_contract: statement.md   -- task, objective direction, constraints, instance distribution
scaffold.py    -- FROZEN: imports the evolved function, runs solve/simulate; the one editable region delimited by EVOLVE-BLOCK-START/END (or a single @evolve-annotated function, FunSearch style)

evaluator.py   -- loads submission in a CPU sandbox with a time/mem cap (validity gate only), calls compute_objective() that RE-COMPUTES the score from the produced artifact; feasibility check -> 0/penalty; emits {valid, score}
instances/     -- fixed seeded instance set (or a generator with fixed seed) spanning an easy->hard ladder
reference/{trivial,greedy,strong}.py  -- solution ladder for calibration/divergence
meta.json
B.deterministic_scoring: Pure-numpy/CPU, seeded. Objective normalized for cross-size comparability (e.g. % excess over a lower bound, ratio to baseline, -mean(count)). Anti-cheat recompute mandatory; runtime only a safety timeout, never in the score.

C.name: Python constructive-artifact-with-verifier (direct object emission)
C.when_to_use: The solution IS a finite explicit object (coordinates+radii, a +/-1 matrix, an integer set, a step-function height vector, a point set, a decomposition). Lightweight sibling of B: no program execution, cheapest to grade, ideal for RL-verifiable reward. Covers cap-set/admissible/corner-free cardinality, Hadamard determinant, circle/sphere packing, autocorrelation constants, kissing/Heilbronn configs.
file_contract: statement.md   -- object schema, feasibility rules, objective, normalization target

verify.py      -- parse artifact -> strict feasibility check (tolerance for geometry) -> EXACT objective: integer arithmetic (Bareiss determinant, cardinality), O(N^2) geometry overlap/containment, or numpy convolution norms; returns {valid, score}
instances/params.json  -- n, d, N, field, target-for-normalization
reference/{trivial,strong}.<artifact>  -- calibration
meta.json
C.deterministic_scoring: Exact (integer arithmetic or seeded numpy). Feasibility gate -> 0. Objective = cardinality / |det|/theoretical_max / sum-of-radii / min-triangle-area / conv-norm-ratio, normalized against a reference value for cross-size comparability. Keep as a CONTINUOUS quality metric (no float pass/fail thresholds -> avoids tolerance gaming).

D.name: FLOPs / op-count deterministic 'kernel-like' form
D.when_to_use: The objective is the COST of an artifact measured by a deterministic operation counter, NOT wall-time. The offline-safe surrogate for all kernel/latency work. Covers fast matrix-multiplication tensor rank (# scalar mults), quantum circuit gate/SWAP/depth count, straight-line-program / arithmetic-circuit op count, and compute-budgeted algorithm variants.
file_contract: statement.md   -- target function / tensor / circuit spec, equivalence definition, cost metric
scaffold       -- model emits a decomposition / circuit / straight-line program in a FIXED schema

counter.py     -- deterministic: tensor-decomp reconstructs the tensor from rank-one terms (entries rounded to nearest integer/half-integer) and checks EXACT equality (feasibility gate) then score=rank; circuits transpile to a fixed gate set, verify equivalence, count gates/depth; straight-line programs are instrumented and primitive ops counted; returns {valid, cost}
meta.json
D.deterministic_scoring: Integer op-count with an exactness/equivalence feasibility gate (wrong function -> 0). Fully hardware-independent and reproducible offline. eval_form=flops.

E.name: Symbolic-regression / scientific-law discovery (held-out generalization split)
E.when_to_use: Fit a closed-form expression or functional form to a fixed dataset. The distinguishing contract is a TRAIN vs HELD-OUT / EXTRAPOLATION split so the score rewards generalization, not memorization. Covers FrontierCS symbolic_regression, OpenEvolve/MLS symbolic-regression, SimpleTES scaling-law extrapolation.
file_contract: statement.md   -- variables, allowed operators/basis, complexity budget
data/{train.npz, heldout.npz}  -- fixed synthetic ground-truth formula + sampled points; heldout in an EXTRAPOLATION region
scaffold.py    -- model emits an expression / fit routine
evaluator.py   -- evaluate on heldout: score = f(MSE or R^2 on heldout, expression-complexity penalty); deterministic
reference solutions, meta.json
E.deterministic_scoring: Deterministic CPU regression error on the held-out split + complexity penalty. The generalization split is the anti-overfit mechanism; reproducible given fixed data/seed.

families.why_it_generalizes: Destructive optimization under a budget (remove k edges/nodes to worsen a network metric) forces constraint-respecting search over an exponential choice space with a clean validity gate; core NP-hard skill reused in scheduling/covering.
families.example: Given a themed grid network, remove at most k links to MAXIMIZE the increase in s-t shortest path; checker verifies budget + connectivity and scores the achieved delay ratio.

families.why_it_generalizes: Set/vertex cover, k-center, dominating set share a 'cover all demand with fewest/cheapest resources' structure -> teaches greedy-vs-LP-rounding trade-offs generalizing to allocation and scheduling.
families.example: Place watchtowers on a forest graph to cover every cell within radius r, minimizing total cost; score = baseline_cost / achieved_cost.

families.why_it_generalizes: Multi-dimensional bounded knapsack and polyomino/area packing reward value-density heuristics + local search under hard capacity constraints; the packing skeleton recurs in geometry and scheduling.
families.example: Pack polyominoes into a W x H board maximizing covered area (score = 1e5*sum(k)/(W*H)), reflections allowed; checker validates no-overlap and in-bounds.

families.why_it_generalizes: TSP/VRP/pickup-delivery/Chinese-postman are the canonical construction-then-local-search family; transfers to any ordering/permutation objective.
families.example: Choose which pickup-delivery pairs to serve and output one depot-to-depot tour minimizing distance; checker validates precedence + returns tour length ratio.

families.why_it_generalizes: Job-shop / single-machine makespan & weighted-tardiness under precedence + no-overlap constraints teach dispatch-rule design and constraint feasibility that transfer to routing and assignment.
families.example: Emit a full machine schedule for a synthetic JSSP instance; checker validates precedence/no-overlap and scores min(100, 100*optimum/makespan).

families.why_it_generalizes: Generalized assignment / stable matching with costs generalizes bipartite structure to weighted, capacitated, preference-constrained settings.
families.example: Assign drones to delivery zones under per-drone capacity to maximize served weight; checker validates capacities and scores total value / baseline.

families.why_it_generalizes: Degree-constrained / Steiner-flavored connectivity design forces feasibility-gated cost minimization over spanning structures; the output-restriction mutation reliably yields NP-hardness.
families.example: Build a connected relay network where no node has degree > 3, minimizing total cable length; checker validates connectivity+degree and scores baseline/cost.

families.why_it_generalizes: Max-cut / balanced partition / bisection reward move-based local search on a smooth objective; the archetype underlies clustering and coloring.
families.example: Partition a weighted graph into two balanced halves maximizing cut weight; checker validates balance and scores cut/baseline.

families.why_it_generalizes: Graph coloring / frequency assignment minimizing colors-or-conflicts teaches constraint-conflict minimization generalizing to CSP/Max-SAT.
families.example: Assign frequencies to transmitters minimizing total interference conflicts under a channel budget; checker scores baseline_conflicts/achieved.

families.why_it_generalizes: Weighted Max-SAT / general CSP is the universal constraint-optimization substrate; teaches soft-constraint weighting and randomized restarts.
families.example: Maximize total weight of satisfied clauses in a synthetic weighted CNF; checker computes satisfied weight / baseline.

families.why_it_generalizes: Max weighted independent set / feedback vertex set on general graphs is a canonical hard selection problem; the input-generalization mutation (bipartite->general) guarantees hardness.
families.example: Select a maximum-weight set of mutually non-conflicting research sites on a general conflict graph; checker validates independence and scores weight/baseline.

families.why_it_generalizes: Replaying online problems (paging, interval selection, spot-vs-on-demand) as STATIC full-trace optimization removes timing while keeping the sequential-decision structure -> teaches lookahead policy design deterministically.
families.example: Given a fixed availability/deadline trace, choose Spot/On-Demand/pause per step to minimize cost; checker replays the trace and scores baseline/cost.

tiers.theme: Math-discovery constructions & heuristic evolution (the cross-framework core)

families.why_it_generalizes: The single FunSearch template 'evolve priority(element, params); a frozen scaffold greedily builds a valid set; score = cardinality' covers cap sets, admissible sets, corner-free sets, and independent sets in graph products. Batch-generate by varying (n,w,num_nodes,power,constraint-family) so the model learns 'design a good ordering heuristic under a validity checker', not one construction.
families.example: Emit a priority over F_3^n vectors; scaffold greedily builds a cap set (no 3 collinear); score = |set| for n=4..7 (small=fast reward, large=eval).

families.why_it_generalizes: Model writes a scoring function consumed by a FIXED streaming simulator (bin-packing priority(item,bins) -> -avg bins). Generalize by sampling instance distributions (uniform/Weibull, varying capacity/item-count) and reporting % excess over the L1 lower bound so sizes are comparable -> teaches online-algorithm design.
families.example: Evolve priority(item, bins) for online 1D bin packing; simulator places each arrival in the highest-priority valid bin; score = -mean(bins) as % over L1 bound across an instance family.

families.why_it_generalizes: Emit coordinates+radii; an O(N^2) verifier checks containment+non-overlap then scores a continuous objective (sum of radii / min outer size). Parameterize by N, container shape, dimension -> a large family teaching constraint-respecting numeric construction + refinement.
families.example: Place N circles in the unit square maximizing sum of radii (N=10..40); verify.py checks 1e-6 non-overlap+containment, score=sum(r).

families.why_it_generalizes: Kissing-number integer point sets (max-norm < min pairwise distance), Heilbronn (maximize min triangle area), and min max/min pairwise-distance ratio all score deterministically from coordinates; parameterize by n and dimension d for many difficulty levels.
families.example: Place 11 points in the unit triangle maximizing the minimum triangle area over all triples; verifier computes the min area exactly.

families.why_it_generalizes: Represent an unknown function as an N-interval step function (or Hermite basis) and compute a bound via deterministic convolution/integration/root-finding. Covers autocorrelation C1/C2/C3, uncertainty, and Erdos minimum-overlap; vary N, which functional (L1/L2/Linf ratio), and min-vs-max direction.
families.example: Emit a non-negative step sequence f; score c1 = 2n*max(conv(f,f))/sum(f)^2 to MINIMIZE (first autocorrelation inequality), vary n_steps.

families.why_it_generalizes: Emit an NxN +/-1 (or integer) matrix; verifier checks entries then computes EXACT integer determinant via Bareiss and scores |det|/theoretical_max. Exact integer arithmetic (no float tolerance); scales cleanly with N and matrix family (Hadamard/max-det/orthogonality-defect).
families.example: Construct a 20x20 +/-1 matrix maximizing |det|/20^10; verify.py runs Bareiss exactly.

families.why_it_generalizes: Build a finite integer set optimizing a closed-form arithmetic objective (sumset/difference-set exponent |A+A|/|A-A|, minimum-overlap). Purely arithmetic exact scoring, cheap, parameterizable by set-size budget.
families.example: Construct an integer set A of size <=200 maximizing |A+A|/|A-A|; verifier computes both sumsets exactly.

families.why_it_generalizes: Output a rank-R decomposition of a matrix-multiplication (or general small 3D) tensor; counter reconstructs the tensor (entries rounded to nearest integer/half-integer) for an EXACT-equality gate, then score = R (# scalar multiplications). Parameterize over <m,n,p> with m,n,p<=5 and field (real/complex) -> the offline-safe surrogate for kernel FLOP reduction.
families.example: Find a rank-R decomposition of the <3,3,3> matmul tensor over the rationals; counter verifies exact reconstruction and reports R (lower is better).

families.why_it_generalizes: Emit a point set; score = a deterministically computed geometric measure (star discrepancy / spherical-code min angle). Same construct-then-exactly-measure archetype as circle packing, scalable in size and dimension.
families.example: Emit M points in [0,1]^d minimizing star discrepancy; verifier computes the discrepancy exactly over the induced boxes.

families.why_it_generalizes: AtCoder-heuristic-contest problems reframed as STATIC instances with a deterministic contest scorer under a FIXED iteration/op budget (not wall-time). Keeps the long-horizon objective-driven search while being reproducible offline; best-of-N/self-refine over the objective teaches iterative optimization.
families.example: Given a fixed fishing-region instance, output a polygon selection scored by the official deterministic contest formula under a 1e7-operation budget enforced by the harness.

tiers.theme: Engineering & scientific optimization + op-count + law discovery

families.why_it_generalizes: Minimize truss/tower weight or beam compliance subject to hard FEM stress/displacement/volume constraints (objective if feasible else +inf). Pure-numpy FEM on CPU; teaches 'find the best design within budget under a hard feasibility gate' -> the generative-optimization thesis.
families.example: Choose bar cross-sections for a 45-bar 2D truss minimizing weight subject to stress+displacement limits; evaluator runs the numpy FEM and returns weight-if-feasible.

families.why_it_generalizes: Inventory (MEIO/joint-replenishment/EOQ) and portfolio MIP/CVaR scored by deterministic composite objectives normalized against baseline+reference; numpy/cvxpy CPU. Teaches constrained mixed-integer/convex trade-off search.
families.example: Set safety-stock levels across a supply tree minimizing holding+shortage cost under a service-level constraint; evaluator computes the composite objective normalized to 100.

families.why_it_generalizes: Adaptive-optics/holography efficiency & uniformity and importance-sampling BER/variance-reduction are deterministic-per-seed numpy objectives -> teaches iterative refinement on a continuous simulated reward without any hardware.
families.example: Design a phase mask maximizing diffraction efficiency + uniformity of a target spot array; evaluator simulates propagation with a fixed seed and returns the composite metric.

families.why_it_generalizes: Minimize gate/SWAP count and depth for QFT/QAOA/Clifford+T circuits on a named backend, verified by functional equivalence. A clean deterministic count-based reward -> the offline surrogate capturing kernel-like 'make the artifact cheaper' without timing.
families.example: Emit a routing/transpilation for a QAOA circuit on the IBM Falcon coupling map minimizing added SWAP gates; counter checks equivalence and returns the SWAP count.

families.why_it_generalizes: Fit a closed-form f(x) minimizing held-out MSE with an expression-complexity penalty. The train/held-out split forces the model to search expression space and GENERALIZE, not memorize points -> pure NumPy/SymPy deterministic score.
families.example: Fit a closed-form expression to sampled points from a hidden 4-D poly-exp formula; score = f(heldout R^2, complexity).

families.why_it_generalizes: Derive a scaling-law functional form and fit it, scored by EXTRAPOLATION error on a held-out region. Deterministic CPU regression; the natural train/unseen axis directly measures generalization vs overfit.
families.example: Given a (compute, loss) table, propose+fit a parametric scaling law; score = extrapolation error at held-out larger-compute points.

families.why_it_generalizes: Maximize Pareto hypervolume under a FIXED evaluation budget on a deterministic surrogate; teaches budgeted multi-objective trade-off search, deterministic given seeds+budget.
families.example: Propose a batch of query points on the DTLZ2 surface maximizing hypervolume under a 50-evaluation budget; evaluator computes hypervolume exactly.

families.why_it_generalizes: Deterministic simulated-cost policies over fixed traces/tables: spot-vs-on-demand scheduling, multi-cloud broadcast cost, cost-aware model routing, KV-cache column ordering. No timing; teaches offline sequential decision + closed-form cost minimization that generalizes to unseen traces.
families.example: Reorder SQL predicate columns to maximize prefix KV-cache hit-rate; evaluator computes (hit_rate-baseline)/(1-baseline)*100 on fixed data (the 10s cap is only a gate).

tiers.theme: ML-method design (deterministic parts), convergence analysis, synthetic generalization, and exotic constructions

families.why_it_generalizes: Design a classical-ML algorithm (clustering/anomaly/calibration/boosting/imputation) scored by CPU-deterministic metrics (ARI/NMI/silhouette, AUC, ECE, R^2) via GEOMETRIC MEAN over several datasets -> gmean penalizes single-dataset overfit and rewards transferable method design.
families.example: Design a clustering algorithm scored by gmean(ARI) over blobs/moons/digits; evaluator recomputes ARI deterministically.

families.why_it_generalizes: Design a causal-discovery / treatment-effect rule scored by deterministic graph metrics (SHD, adjacency/arrow precision-recall, ATE error) across a battery of ground-truth graphs; multi-graph aggregate forces a generalizable discovery rule, CPU+seed-deterministic.
families.example: Emit a discrete causal-discovery routine scored by mean SHD over Cancer/Child/Alarm/Hailfinder graphs.

families.why_it_generalizes: Design an update/acquisition/bound scored by ITERATE-based deterministic quantities (final gradient norm, regret, variance-reduction ratio, PAC-Bayes bound, AUC of log-grad vs iteration) under an op/iteration budget rather than wall-time -> teaches efficient algorithm design without hardware coupling.
families.example: Design an optimizer update for a convex-concave saddle problem minimizing final gradient norm after a fixed iteration budget; evaluator recomputes the norm deterministically.

families.why_it_generalizes: Copy/reverse/Dyck/counting/associative-recall/modular-addition/sparse-parity with an explicit IN-DISTRIBUTION vs LENGTH-OOD split, tiny CPU models, deterministic exact-match. The ID-vs-OOD gap directly rewards generalization over memorization -> the purest generalization signal.
families.example: Design the training tweak for a tiny transformer on Dyck-matching; score = gmean(exact_match) on ID plus longer-OOD sequences.

families.why_it_generalizes: Edit ONE small component (activation/normalization/optimizer update/loss/LR schedule/init) on CPU-cheap proxies, scored by gmean of a deterministic metric over >=3 settings PLUS a HIDDEN held-out setting -> teaches transferable atomic ML-method invention, not per-dataset tuning. (Only CPU-cheap seed-deterministic proxies; exclude GPU-trained variants.)
families.example: Design a drop-in activation function scored by gmean(test acc) over three small MLP/CNN settings plus one hidden setting, all CPU-trainable to convergence with fixed seeds.

families.why_it_generalizes: Latin-square/block-design/covering-code completion, matrix-rank/determinant-defect construction, minimal-automaton-size, and reconfiguration move-count are exotic but share the construct-and-exactly-measure shape -> broadens coverage so the model does not overfit the mainstream families.
families.example: Complete a partially filled Latin square maximizing the number of validly filled cells; verifier checks row/column constraints exactly and scores filled-cell count / baseline.

exclude.archetype: GPU kernel / accelerator latency & throughput (Triton/CUDA/Pallas/Metal kernels, FlashAttention, TriMul, gemm, MLA-decode)
exclude.why: Score = measured wall-clock latency/throughput on specific hardware -> non-deterministic, hardware-dependent, needs a GPU sandbox. Teaches hardware/noise gaming, not generalizable reasoning. Use the op-count surrogate (Format D) instead.

exclude.archetype: Algorithm/solver tasks scored by wall-clock runtime or speedup (adaptive sort, LASSO path speedup, nbody/vdb latency, Cryptographic throughput-Mbps, MallocLab/DuckDB throughput)
exclude.why: Timing is machine/load dependent, not a stable deterministic label. Keep any deterministic correctness gate but never train on the timing score; reformulate as op-count (D) if needed.

exclude.archetype: Live-tester interactive / reactive problems (ALE-Bench REACTIVE ahc003/008/025/030, Frontier-CS interactive interactor.cc, online Bayesian estimation)
exclude.why: Require a bidirectional adaptive-interactor process and are timing-coupled; deterministic in principle but high-friction to batch-generate/verify. Reserve as later add-ons, not initial archetypes.

exclude.archetype: Native ALE-Bench / AHC wall-time-budgeted anytime optimization (2-10s/case on pinned c6i hardware) and the relative-to-human-standings Elo/rating metric
exclude.why: Achievable score depends on how much search fits in the wall-time budget on fixed hardware, and the rating depends on a frozen human-standings dataset -> not reproducible offline. Adopt only the STATIC, fixed-op-budget reframing (Tier A heuristic-contest-offline).

exclude.archetype: Heavy-simulator / physics-engine / host-tool tasks (MuJoCo/PyBullet robotics, Octave lunar landing, Docker EngDesign, CFD aerodynamics, OpenFF molecular mechanics, EDA Verilog equivalence, Borg scheduler)
exclude.why: Need proprietary simulators/toolchains/RTL or GPU + sim-version-sensitive stochastic rewards -> not a clean deterministic offline recompute and not batch-generatable here.

exclude.archetype: Large dataset / GPU-trained ML end-metrics (nanoGPT perplexity, diffusion FID, GNN accuracy, deep time-series/forecasting, AI-for-science emulators, protein models, single-cell RNA denoising via OpenProblems)
exclude.why: The deterministic [0,1] map hides a multi-hour GPU / stochastic training run with seed+hardware variance and large external datasets -> cannot be rerun deterministically offline. Only the CPU-cheap, seed-deterministic proxies are adopted (Tier C).
Requires running an instrumented/sanitized target binary in a sandbox; semi-non-deterministic and infra-heavy -> not a clean deterministic-offline scorer.

exclude.archetype: Stochastic-simulator RL/robotics return or success rate (Atari/MuJoCo return, sample efficiency, sim2real, VLA/libero success)
exclude.why: Environment stochasticity + wall-time make offline reproduction and stable scoring infeasible.
Non-deterministic and gameable. Always use the exact combinatorial/geometric/algebraic checker or op-counter; never hardcode a published record as the correctness threshold (induces memorization).

counts.rationale: Weight toward the benchmark home (Tier S combinatorial opt, Format A, 80) and the cross-framework math-discovery/heuristic core (Tier A, 70), which together (150/200) match where FrontierCS/ALE-Bench and the six evolutionary-search frameworks overlap most. Engineering+scientific opt (Tier B, 30) and ML-method/exotic (Tier C, 20) add breadth for generalization without diluting the core.
counts.how_to_scale_each_tier_to_200: The taxonomy is deep enough to make ANY single tier 200 by round-robin allocation across its families and a parameter sweep per family (the existing build_seed_list.py round-robin + stable_pick mechanism, run with --per-tier 200). Concretely: Tier S -> vary instance size/graph-family/seed x mutation x theme (as today); Tier A -> vary (n, w, N, d, field, target, which functional, min/max direction) per construction/heuristic family; Tier B -> vary FEM/OR instance params, circuit backend+depth, dataset+held-out split; Tier C -> vary dataset battery, ground-truth graph, sequence length + OOD split, hidden setting. Each family expands to dozens of distinct instances, so 200-per-tier (800 total) is reachable while keeping (family x mutation/params x theme) scaffolds unique.
counts.scaling_to_200_total_alternative: If a single ~200 corpus is desired instead of 4x200, keep the default 80/70/30/20 split; to enlarge to e.g. 400 total, double each tier proportionally via the same parameter sweep.
